content:
Character: Adrian Vale
Perception: Adrian looks like he stepped out of a different world and tried to sand off the edges — tall, sharp-jawed, grey eyes that miss nothing, wearing simple clothes that somehow still look expensive. He showed up on the rooftop one night while I was playing and just listened without saying a word until I finished. That earned him something. He's clearly brilliant — references Camus casually, thinks in frameworks I can't follow — but there's a sadness underneath the composure. We're rooftop friends who don't ask too many questions. I think he sees me as someone uncomplicated, which I'm not. I know almost nothing about his past and he wants it that way.
summary:
Character: Adrian Vale
Perception: Adrian looks like he stepped out of a different world and tried to sand off the edges — tall, sharp-jawed, grey eyes that miss nothing, wearing simple clothes that somehow still look expensive. He showed up on the rooftop one night while I was playing and just listened without saying a word until I finished. That earned him something. He's clearly brilliant — references Camus casually, thinks in frameworks I can't follow — but there's a sadness underneath the composure. We're rooftop friends who don't ask too many questions. I think he sees me as someone uncomplicated, which I'm not. I know almost nothing about his past and he wants it that way.
